target.name: Speedata
target.founder: Jonathan Friedmann, Dan Charash, Rafi Shalom, Itai Incze, Yoav Etsion, Dani Voitsechov

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $114M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $11.0B.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $5B

private.name: Positron AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Speedata is a Series B semiconductor company that has raised $114M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 11 peers across public and private markets in the chips and semiconductors sector, including 3 public names (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private companies. The peer set spans both earlier and later stages, with adjacent reference points including SambaNova Systems ($11.0B, Series A), Etched ($5.0B, Series B), d-Matrix ($2.0B, Series C), Positron AI ($1.0B, Series B), and DEEPX ($529M, Series C).

Two valuation methods are applied. Stage-progression anchored to the $114M total raised uses a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implying $217M. Peer-median methodology across the five same-sector reference points yields a median valuation of $2.0B, with a range spanning $529M to $11.0B. The wide peer dispersion reflects heterogeneity in company maturity, commercialization stage, and market positioning within the semiconductor frontier-tech landscape.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $184M (low) to $2.3B (high), with a median of $1.1B. The substantial gap between stage-progression and peer-median approaches reflects tension between Speedata's current capital base and the valuations achieved by comparable-stage peers in this capital-intensive sector. Key uncertainties include product validation progress, revenue traction, and competitive differentiation relative to the sampled peer set.
